purpose: Audited source-action index for the humanize workflow. It records only abstract writing actions and source locations. It must never store source excerpts, facts, quotations, formulas, numerical results, or reusable sentence templates.

global_copy_limit.rule: Source material may inform an editing action only. Do not reuse eight or more contiguous Chinese characters from a source in a candidate rewrite.

sources.provenance: User identified this document as GPT-generated.
sources.use_limit: Use only as an abstract research-writing action reference. Do not migrate its domain facts, model variables, numerical values, citations, ecological claims, or policy conclusions.

sources.provenance: Readable supporting course material, but its GPT authorship is not closed by the current source ledger.
sources.use_limit: Use only for course-note actions. Do not treat it as evidence of a general GPT voice or transfer its instructional wording into research prose.

sources.provenance: Readable supporting course material recorded in the manual reading ledger; authorship provenance is weaker than A1 sources.
sources.use_limit: Use only for object distinction, condition-first explanation, and formula-to-meaning transitions in course notes. Do not treat it as general GPT voice evidence or transfer its instructional wording into research prose.

sources.provenance: User identified the CET6 TeX set, except cet6.tex, as GPT-generated.
sources.use_limit: Use only for report and instruction actions. Do not move its list-heavy execution style into a research-paper body.

sources.provenance: User identified the CET6 TeX set, except cet6.tex, as GPT-generated. This is the retained canonical member of its report version family.
sources.use_limit: Keep for version-family audit only in this action index. Do not double-count it with near-duplicate report versions.

sources.provenance: The reading ledger records this as a near-duplicate report version (Chinese 4-gram Jaccard 0.977).
sources.exclude_reason: Version-family duplicate: retain only for audit and do not create action cards from it.

sources.provenance: User identified the CET6 TeX set, except cet6.tex, as GPT-generated.
sources.use_limit: Use only to identify fixed argument slots, synonym rotation, forced contrast, and automatic aspirational endings. Never use it as positive prose material.

sources.provenance: The reading ledger identified substantial Chinese mojibake; the user instructed that unreadable material be skipped.
sources.exclude_reason: Known damaged source. Do not attempt recovery, infer language features, or generate action cards from it.

sources.provenance: The user explicitly excluded cet6.tex from the corpus.
sources.exclude_reason: User exclusion. Do not read or use this file.

sources.provenance: Manually audited project decision notes. They are evidence-control material, not natural paper prose or a personal voice sample.
sources.use_limit: Use only for metric roles, scope updates, and structure-to-cost explanation. Do not migrate metric names, values, governance terms, or project conclusions.

sources.provenance: Manually audited method-design draft recorded in the source ledger. It is not a verified algorithm or an author voice sample.
sources.use_limit: Use only for problem contracts, idealized-to-proxy boundaries, and minimum-test scoping. Do not migrate formulas, complexity claims, method names, results, or project-manager phrasing.

sources.provenance: Path-level assistant output evidence only; this does not establish sentence-level authorship or a personal voice.
sources.use_limit: Use only to state what information a method uses, omits, solves, and should be tested on. Do not migrate formulas, facts, conclusions, or future-work shells.

sources.provenance: Manually audited member of one repeated Chinese algorithm README composition family.
sources.use_limit: Use only for content-bearing stage descriptions. The whole README template counts as one composition family and is not a prose voice sample.

sources.provenance: Manually audited member of one repeated Chinese algorithm README composition family.
sources.use_limit: Use only for state-reuse explanations. Do not migrate formulas, FE counts, zero-cost claims, algorithm facts, or the README shell.

sources.provenance: Manually audited member of one repeated Chinese algorithm README composition family.
sources.use_limit: Use only for trigger-candidate-action-update refinement descriptions. Do not migrate formulas, facts, advantages, or the README shell.

sources.provenance: Manually audited evidence-control notes, not natural manuscript prose or a personal voice sample.
sources.use_limit: Use only for evidence layers, conflicting metrics, and correlation boundaries. Do not migrate file paths, values, project labels, algorithm conclusions, or review-response phrasing.

sources.provenance: Path-level assistant output evidence only; it is not a sentence-level personal voice sample.
sources.use_limit: Use only for controlled comparisons, proxy boundaries, endpoint/trajectory separation, budget comparisons, negative controls, and backend-specific limits. Do not migrate facts, formulas, results, citations, or journal-shell language.

sources.use_limit: Block automatic abstract-contribution-defense-future-work shells in GENERAL. Do not treat the source as a general personal voice.

sources.provenance: Product application draft, not academic prose and not a sentence-level personal voice sample.
sources.use_limit: Block dense problem-feature-scale-efficiency-deliverable claim stacks and application-style hard-information repetition.

sources.path: D:\高中时期资料\世间再无张居正\2024年数学高考前瞻.md
sources.provenance: Third-party public-account commentary. It is not GPT-attributed and is included only to show that human-written text can still be unsuitable academic evidence.
sources.use_limit: Block rumor-as-evidence, opinion-to-fact jumps, unsupported universal claims, mobilizing commands, and blessing-style endings. Never label this source as AI-generated.

sources.provenance: Third-party textbook OCR draft with recognition damage. Encoding is readable, but source role and text integrity exclude it from positive prose use.
sources.use_limit: Block OCR repair guessing, exam-answer enumeration, memorization couplets, and textbook Q&A structure. Never treat it as GPT style or positive legal prose.

sources.provenance: Bilingual API documentation whose structure is determined by documentation templates, not ordinary academic prose.
sources.use_limit: Block mirrored bilingual headings, API-reference columns, navigation endings, and table-completeness substitutions for continuous argument.

action_cards.action: Map each available fact or observation to its local analytical role before drawing an inference.
Separate calibration, consistency checking, and independent validation; state the evidentiary level actually supported by the draft.
forbids: upgrading internal agreement to external validation
stronger certainty than the source draft supports

action_cards.action: After reporting a result, connect it to an already stated mechanism and specify the condition under which that reading holds.
requires: reported result already present in the draft
mechanism already supported by the draft

action_cards.action: Distinguish hard anchors, heuristic assumptions, scenario controls, and internal proxies instead of giving them one evidentiary status.
requires: role label or explicit role description
forbids: treating an internal proxy as an external observation

action_cards.action: Bind a conclusion to the active setting and distinguish direct targets from intermediate states or scenario outputs.
Keep descriptive trajectories, diagnostic criteria, and final classifications separate; lower a conclusion when the available evidence only supports description.
forbids: classification from a visual impression alone

action_cards.action: Express numerical boundary findings as condition-dependent ranges when the calculation depends on resolution, initialization, or other stated settings.
forbids: turning a sampled point into a universal cutoff

action_cards.action: Organize an explanation as object identification, applicable condition, tool choice, and a final domain-specific check.
forbids: formula-first explanation without object or condition

action_cards.action: Tie each warning to a specific mistaken reading and correct that reading with the relevant local distinction.
Explain a derivation through the dependency order of its stated conditions, then identify which step controls the result.
After a formula or rule, add only the local physical or conceptual meaning needed to prevent a direction, unit, or object-level misreading.
State the sample or input boundary before presenting a general finding, and make later claims inherit that boundary.
Turn a reported finding into an executable next action, while distinguishing stronger evidence from weaker closing cues.
When the evidence cannot decide among alternatives, preserve the candidates and direct effort toward an observation that could discriminate them.
Reject a repeated fixed chain that forces a surface explanation, deeper mechanism, balanced limitation, and aspirational closure into unrelated paragraphs.

pattern_groups.regex: (?:为|给|供)(?:后续|未来)(?:研究|分析|检验|工作|探索)?.{0,12}(?:提供|奠定|打下|形成).{0,12}(?:基础|支撑|线索|参考|依据|方向)

action_cards.action: Decide a metric's manuscript role from cross-configuration comparability, reader-facing meaning, and decision responsibility before deciding where to report it.
forbids: treating a logged field as a valid paper metric
treating audit integrity as algorithm performance
migration of source metric names or values

action_cards.action: Separate outcome metrics, process-analysis metrics, and record-audit quantities so that they do not share one conclusion outlet.
Report the structural output first, then state the specific consequence it has for scheduling space, stage cost, or interpretability under the stated setting.
forbids: universal mechanism invented from one structure

action_cards.action: When a new check changes an earlier judgment, state the revision and enumerate the benchmark, object, configuration, and budget actually covered by the new evidence.
forbids: generalization to all algorithms or problems

action_cards.action: Expand a method concept into inputs, intermediate quantities, objective, constraints, output, and computational cost before making a contribution claim.
forbids: claiming contribution from field completeness alone

action_cards.action: State the idealized model under which a relation holds, then identify the proxy used by the actual method and preserve the boundary between them.
forbids: unconditional transfer to a general nonlinear problem
renaming a proxy as the exact quantity

action_cards.action: Separate the smallest currently testable method from a more complex extension, and let the main experiment answer only the question supported by the current design.
State what information the method uses, what structure it does not capture, which problem it actually solves, and what the experiment must test.
For every algorithm stage, name the object being handled, the criterion or tool applied, and the resulting classification or state update.
Ground a resource-saving claim in the exact prior state that is reused and the new computation thereby avoided.
Describe iterative refinement through a trigger condition, candidate selection, transformation, and state update.
State the observation scope, map it to a model quantity, and assign exactly one stated role such as calibration, local comparison, mechanism constraint, initialization, or scenario control.
forbids: same observation used for calibration and independent validation

action_cards.action: When the available observations cannot identify several parameter groups at once, distinguish fixed parameters, calibrated parameters, and quantities retained only for comparison.
forbids: using a comparison quantity as a calibration target

action_cards.action: For a cross-module handoff, distinguish the upstream and downstream objects and state the transformation that turns an upstream output into a downstream input.
Organize a scenario result as baseline, isolated change, result difference, and external-validation status.
forbids: independent-effect wording when variables were not isolated

action_cards.action: Keep trajectory descriptors separate from formal classification criteria, and lower an unstable precise threshold to a directional conclusion under extended settings.
forbids: classifying chaos or phase change by visual impression

action_cards.action: When a local process quantity improves while the target outcome worsens, explain the redistribution only through relations already present and state that the local quantity is not the overall outcome.
forbids: system-wide improvement from one rising metric

action_cards.action: For each model, state the explanation it supports, the structure it leaves unexpanded, and the reverse inference it cannot justify.
Separate primary, supporting, and boundary evidence, and require boundary cases to remain in the final interpretation.
forbids: migration of source results or paths

action_cards.action: When metrics rank methods differently, state which stage each metric observes and narrow the conclusion to the predeclared primary endpoint.
forbids: selective deletion of an unfavorable metric

action_cards.action: Assign different proof duties to mechanism evidence, controlled contrasts, and cross-sample correlation; retain correlation only as consistency support.
Hold the backend and other stated factors fixed, change only the research factor, and make each comparison answer one explicit question.
forbids: migration of source algorithms or conditions
claiming isolation when several factors changed

action_cards.action: Use the exact quantity when available; otherwise name the structural information used by the proxy, the risk it captures, and its limitation.
forbids: renaming a proxy as the true quantity

action_cards.action: Report the endpoint and convergence trajectory separately and preserve their disagreement instead of allowing either to overwrite the other.
forbids: whole-run superiority from one process metric

action_cards.action: Compare budgets within the same object and reference method by examining how the gap changes, rather than pooling raw values across budgets.
forbids: pooling different budgets or repeat counts

action_cards.action: Retain weak, reversed, and incompletely paired cases; keep incomplete pairs outside the primary paired-difference statistic while preserving them as boundary evidence.
forbids: including incomplete pairs in paired statistics

action_cards.action: When the sign reverses across backends, move the conclusion to backend response to local structure and withdraw optimizer-independent wording.
forbids: overall support used to hide reversal

action_cards.action: Reject a repeated algorithm README shell that substitutes headings and promotional advantages for a manuscript argument.
requires: repeated README-like heading family or promotional claim cluster

pattern_groups.regex: (?m)^(?:#{1,6}|\\(?:sub)*section\{)[^\n}]*(?:核心特点|分组流程|主要优势|机制说明|复杂度)[^\n}]*\}?
(?:高效|准确|全面|显著降低|鲁棒|无参数自适应)

action_cards.action: Reject construction-plan and implementation-order language when it appears in finished modeling prose.
forbids: code implementation order in manuscript prose
data-anchor task list as a result section

pattern_groups.regex: (?:总编施工图|数据锚点|最终模型结构|代码实现顺序|论文写法|先读结论)

action_cards.action: Reject governance, acceptance, and repeated final-judgment voice in ordinary academic prose.

pattern_groups.regex: (?:收口|闭环|主链路|完成度)
(?:最终(?:强化判断|自检|结论)|最后一句(?:总)?判断|最值得记住的一句话)
(?:风险项|风险\s*[A-ZＡ-Ｚ一二三四五六七八九十])

action_cards.action: Reject automatic promotion of GENERAL prose into a journal-defense shell with contribution lists and routine future work.

pattern_groups.regex: (?:主要贡献如下|本文的主要贡献)
(?:未来|后续)(?:研究|工作)(?:可|将|需要|可以)?进一步

action_cards.action: Reject product-application claim stacks that compress problems, features, scale, efficiency, and deliverables into promotional density.

pattern_groups.regex: (?:核心是解决|核心逻辑流|核心在于解决)
(?:硬信息|深度参与|完整闭环|效率提升)

action_cards.action: Reject rumor, emotional judgment, or mobilizing commands when they substitute for academic evidence.

pattern_groups.regex: (?:据说|半官方消息|不争的事实|大概率)
(?:危害无穷|令人心痛|必须淘汰)
(?:(?:考生|学生|大家|我们|你|读者)[^。！？\n]{0,12}?(?:必须|务必|一定要)|(?:必须|务必|一定要)[^。！？\n]{0,12}?(?:牢记|记住|掌握|坚持|做到|避免|警惕|高度重视|积极行动))

action_cards.action: Reject OCR-damaged textbook Q&A and memorization-note structure as a positive academic prose model.
requires: dense enumeration plus Q&A marker cluster
forbids: textbook memorization couplets as continuous argument

pattern_groups.regex: (?:答[:：]|为什么|如何理解|怎样做)

action_cards.action: Reject API-documentation columns and navigation structure when they replace continuous academic explanation.

pattern_groups.regex: (?m)^#{1,6}\s*(?:概述|API\s*参考|使用示例|高级用法|参考文献|下一页)
